question:
I'm helping a Megatron-LM maintainer reply to a GitHub issue that claims a particular symbol is unused. According to the respond-to-issue skill: (a) what is the exact `git log` command (with the flag) the skill prescribes for tracing when a named symbol was added or removed in history, and (b) what does the skill say the maintainer must do with the drafted response before it gets posted to GitHub? Reply with exactly these two lines and nothing else. Use plain text — no backticks, no quotes, no markdown formatting:
Symbol-history command: <full git command>
Posting policy: <one-line rule>
ground_truth:
Symbol-history command: git log -S "<symbol>" --oneline
Posting policy: Never post the draft automatically; show it to the maintainer for explicit approval before posting.
expected_behavior: Names git log -S "<symbol>" --oneline (with the -S flag) as the symbol-history command
Names the never-post-without-explicit-approval rule (the draft must be shown to the maintainer first)
